reason: The only residual finding is a missing hint `nablarch.fw.web.handler.HttpErrorHandler` in s3. The FQCN already appears in the s3 section body (XML code examples), and `HttpErrorHandler` is already present as a hint in s1. Retrieval via the general class name will still surface the configuration section. The impact is low — reduced hint precision, not missing content.

findings_assessment.justification: The FQCN `nablarch.fw.web.handler.HttpErrorHandler` is present in the s3 section body (XML code examples), so content-level retrieval still works. s1 already includes `HttpErrorHandler` as a hint, providing a retrieval path for general queries about this handler. The missing hint would only matter if an agent performs strict hint-only matching against the fully-qualified web-package class name — a narrow edge case. Phase E failed to apply the fix (s3 hints in the output are identical to Phase D), but the omission has no meaningful user-facing consequence given the redundancy in s1 hints and s3 body content.
